markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Extract JavaScript modules, functions, classes, exports, imports, and test blocks. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: JavaScriptSymbolExtractor leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: JavaScriptSymbolExtractor is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: Same-name collision (shared with MT-098/099 upsert merge): JS allows a `class Foo{}` and a `function Foo(){}`/`const Foo=()=>{}` in different scopes; the extractor scopes only under class_declaration (JS_SCOPE_KINDS, symbols.rs:424), so two same-named top-level decls map to symbol_path `Foo` -> identical entity_key -> silent merge at knowledge.rs:3336.

ts_const_binding (reused for JS, symbols.rs:439) returns only the FIRST function-valued declarator, so `const a=()=>{}, b=()=>{}` drops `b`; and a `const handler = function(){}` reassigned later is indexed once at the first binding (later reassignment invisible).

Methods only scoped under class_declaration: an object-literal method (`const obj = { run() {} }`) is not a method_definition under a class, so it is not indexed - acceptable (matches conservative contract) but means JS object-method navigation is silently absent, not flagged.
evasion_probes: Read extract_javascript (symbols.rs:426-460): mirrors the TS path with a narrower node set; no merge-awareness, so collisions behave identically to MT-099. Confirmed Store.put scopes correctly under class (delegate test javascript_extracts_functions_classes_const_arrows).
Confirmed .jsx/.mjs/.cjs route to the JavaScript grammar (parser.rs:103-109) which accepts JSX.
No panic path: all name lookups are Option-guarded; empty names skipped (symbols.rs:443).

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG/unit test green (knowledge_code_index_tests 13 + nav_api 2 + 59 unit, 0 fail).

evidence: Hardened b0266f35: JS class+function same-name disambiguated; all const declarators extracted.

findings.title: Positional-ordinal churn (shared with 098/099); re-confirm all const declarators are extracted, not just the first function-valued one
findings.evidence: JS class/function same-name disambiguated via kind tag (symbols.rs); the V1 finding that ts_const_binding returned only the FIRST function-valued declarator was claimed fixed (all const declarators extracted) - worth a spot re-confirm in the validator since the multi-declarator path is subtle.
findings.fix: Same edit-stable discriminator as 098; keep a test asserting `const a=()=>{}, b=()=>{}` yields both a and b.

missing_tests: test: multi-declarator const (a, b both function-valued) extracts both symbols
adversarial_review_v2.note: VERIFIED FIXED from V1 (b0266f35): JS class+function same-name disambiguated; all const declarators claimed extracted. Residual: positional-ordinal churn + suggest a multi-declarator regression test.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-100 requires a Handshake-native JavaScript symbol extractor (functions, classes, methods, exported const/function, test blocks) with stable entity keys stored through the shared PostgreSQL/EventLedger knowledge authority. The committed code implements this in extract_javascript (symbols.rs:582-637), resolves the prior same-name/multi-declarator merge bugs, and proves it end-to-end against real managed PostgreSQL.
findings: symbols.rs:582-637 extract_javascript: real, conservative extractor. Handles function_declaration/generator_function_declaration, class_declaration, method_definition (under class scope JS_SCOPE_KINDS), lexical/variable_declaration via ts_const_bindings, and it/test/describe test blocks via ts_js_test_case_symbol_path. Names Option-guarded, empty names skipped (line 616) - no panic path.

V1 adversarial MEDIUM findings are genuinely fixed in commit b0266f35: (a) class Foo + function Foo no longer collide - each top-level decl carries a kind-tag disambiguator via ts_js_kind_tag (symbols.rs:485-499, 632); (b) ts_const_bindings (symbols.rs:543-574) now extracts EVERY variable_declarator, not just the first function-valued one, and tags non-function bindings as Constant; (c) any residual same-base-key duplicates get a stable dupN ordinal via assign_collision_ordinals (symbols.rs:140-165).

entity_key (symbols.rs:116-132) appends the ~discriminator only when present; nav simple-name parser strips it (per commit msg), so name/path lookup is unaffected while distinct symbols stay distinct.

Real unit proof present and specific: javascript_extracts_functions_classes_const_arrows (970-988) asserts Function/Class/Method/Function/Component kinds; javascript_class_and_function_same_name_do_not_collide (1124-1137) and lowercase_function_and_const_same_name_do_not_collide (1139-1149) assert assert_unique_keys; multi-declarator coverage via typescript_multi_declarator_const_extracts_all (1086-1104) exercises the shared ts_const_bindings path (the V2-requested regression test).

Real managed-PostgreSQL proof (sub_rule_2): tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs mt112 (knowledge_pg() harness, line 312; SKIPs loudly when PG absent, never silently green) indexes a JS fixture app/store.js through the shared knowledge_* authority tables and asserts the disambiguated JS entity key landed: 'javascript:app/store.js#compute~value' (line 417). No SQLite/Docker/external-LSP path.
sub_rule_1: grep of symbols.rs found zero todo!()/unimplemented!()/unreachable!()/Unavailable/'pending MT-'/'deferred to follow' on any path. No deferred-live escape.

Conservative-scope limitation (object-literal methods like const obj={run(){}} not indexed) is documented in the adversarial review as intentional/acceptable for the contract, not a defect; it does not contradict the MT acceptance criteria. Spec anchor 2.3.13.11 (code-index relationship/citation model) is present in the current v02.194 bundle (spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md), confirming spec alignment.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
